name: Stages
description: Six-segment generator. Each segment configurable as ramp · hold (step) · attack/decay via mode button. Chained segments form envelopes, LFOs, sequencers, or complex multi-stage shapes.

controls.name: Segment Sliders (×6)
controls.description: Main level/time control per segment. In ramp mode = duration. In hold mode = voltage level. Full-size 60mm sliders.
controls.detail: CH 1–6 · touch-sensitive firmware detects handling for easter-egg modes

controls.name: Shape / Time Pot (×6)
controls.description: Sub-knob per segment. Ramp mode: shape curve (expo→lin→log). Hold mode: depends on segment position (glide/level jitter).
controls.detail: CCW: expo · noon: linear · CW: log · also sets step hold-time in sequencer chain

controls.name: Mode Button (×6)
controls.description: Cycles segment role: ramp (green) · hold/step (orange) · attack-decay (red blinking) · free-running LFO (when no gate, LED breathes).
controls.detail: Green = ramp · orange = hold/step · red = decaying envelope segment · long-press = chain grouping toggle

controls.name: Segment Chaining
controls.description: Adjacent segments can be grouped into one multi-stage function by patching the GATE of segment N to segment N−1's GATE or leaving GATE jacks empty in a contiguous span.
controls.detail: Group LEDs between segments show chained vs. independent segments

inputs.name: GATE / TRIG (×6)
inputs.description: Per-segment gate/trigger. Starts the segment. Patching GATE of segment N breaks any chain to its left, so GATE placement defines group boundaries.

inputs.name: LEVEL CV (×6)
inputs.description: Per-segment CV modulating the slider value (level or time).

outputs.name: OUT (×6)
outputs.description: Per-segment output. In a chain, outputs of earlier segments reflect the in-progress function; the group's "final" output is the last chained segment.

behaviors.name: Auto-chaining from gate patching
behaviors.description: Contiguous segments with no GATE patched form a single chain. A GATE cable patched at segment N starts a new chain beginning at N. This is how you build any length of envelope/sequence from the six segments.

behaviors.name: Ramp mode (green)
behaviors.description: Segment produces a ramp from its predecessor's endpoint to its own slider level over the time set by the segment. Shape knob selects curve (expo/lin/log).

behaviors.name: Hold/Step mode (orange)
behaviors.description: Segment holds a constant voltage set by its slider. In a chain, this turns the segment into a sequencer step. Shape knob acts as hold-time multiplier.

behaviors.name: Attack/Decay mode (red blink)
behaviors.description: Segment becomes an envelope: ramps up to slider level (attack) then decays. Shape knob controls decay curve. Triggered by GATE rising edge.

behaviors.name: Free-running LFO
behaviors.description: An unpatched chain loops continuously. Chaining multiple ramp segments + a hold produces complex asymmetric LFO shapes — any waveform you can break into six linear segments.

behaviors.name: Sequencer chain
behaviors.description: Set every segment to hold mode, leave GATEs unpatched except for segment 1 (clock). Stages advances to the next step on each clock pulse, producing a CV sequencer with sliders as the step values.

behaviors.name: ADSR as chain
behaviors.description: Four adjacent ramp segments with gate on segment 1 form a full ADSR: attack to peak, decay to sustain level, hold sustain while gate high, release on gate low.

behaviors.name: Ouroboros easter egg
behaviors.description: Alternate firmware makes Stages behave as six harmonically-related quadrature oscillators — each segment outputs a sine wave at a harmonic of segment 1's frequency. Accessed by button-hold at power-on.

behaviors.name: Segmented generator chain mode
behaviors.description: Default firmware is already the segment generator. The green/orange/red per-segment LEDs show role; when touched, the slider glows white to indicate the user is adjusting that segment.

behaviors.name: Slew mode via ramp-only chain
behaviors.trigger: single ramp segment with GATE held
behaviors.description: A ramp segment receiving a fluctuating LEVEL CV acts as a portamento/slew generator — output slews toward the LEVEL target at the time set by the slider.

patchIdeas.name: Six-step CV sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: All six segments in hold mode · clock → GATE 1 (advances through chain) · sliders set step values · OUT 6 feeds VCO V/OCT via quantizer · Shape pots add per-step hold-time variation · quick way to a 6-step CV lane
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* All segments: Hold (orange)

- Clock (Out) c> Stages (GATE 1)
- Stages (OUT 6) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Clock (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Full ADSR envelope
patchIdeas.patch: Segments 1–4 in ramp mode (attack, decay, sustain-hold, release) · keyboard gate → GATE 1 · Shape pots sculpt curves · OUT 4 → VCA CV · full-featured ADSR built from a 4-segment chain
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1: Ramp (attack)
* Seg 2: Ramp (decay)
* Seg 3: Hold (sustain)
* Seg 4: Ramp (release)

- Keyboard (Gate) g> Stages (GATE 1)
- Stages (OUT 4) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Complex-shape LFO
patchIdeas.patch: All six segments in ramp mode · no GATE patched (free-running chain) · alternating slider levels create a six-segment non-sinusoidal LFO · Shape pots add curves · OUT 6 as main LFO output for filter modulation
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* All segments: Ramp (green)
* All GATEs: unpatched (free-running chain)

- Stages (OUT 6) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Two independent envelopes
patchIdeas.patch: Segments 1–3 = envelope A (gate 1) · segments 4–6 = envelope B (gate 4) · each chain attacks to peak and decays · GATE 4 patched starts second group · two envelopes in one module for dual-VCA setups
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1-3: Ramp (env A)
* Seg 4-6: Ramp (env B)

- Gate A (Out) g> Stages (GATE 1)
- Gate B (Out) g> Stages (GATE 4)
- Stages (OUT 3) >> VCA 1 (CV)
- Stages (OUT 6) >> VCA 2 (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Slew / portamento
patchIdeas.patch: Segment 1 in ramp mode · GATE 1 held high via +5V offset · LEVEL CV 1 fed from stepped sequencer pitch CV · slider sets slew time · OUT 1 = smoothed pitch CV feeding VCO · one-segment slew limiter
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1: Ramp (slew)

- Offset (+5V) g> Stages (GATE 1) // held high
- Sequencer (Pitch) >> Stages (LEVEL CV 1)
- Stages (OUT 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Burst generator
patchIdeas.patch: 3 ramp segments + 3 hold segments · Cycle via self-patching OUT 6 → GATE 1 via Schmitt-like threshold · produces repeating burst of 3 envelope hits whenever external gate is high · good for drum rolls and gated rhythmic bursts
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1-3: Ramp
* Seg 4-6: Hold

- External Gate (Out) g> Stages (GATE 1)
- Stages (OUT 6) t> Stages (GATE 1) // self-retrig for burst
- Stages (OUT 3) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Sequencer + envelopes combo
patchIdeas.patch: Segments 1–3 hold mode = 3-step CV sequencer · segments 4–6 ramp mode = 3-stage envelope · clock into GATE 1 advances sequencer · trigger into GATE 4 fires envelope · one Stages = sequence and shape a voice
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Seg 1-3: Hold (sequencer)
* Seg 4-6: Ramp (envelope)

- Clock (Out) c> Stages (GATE 1)
- Clock (Out) t> Stages (GATE 4)
- Stages (OUT 3) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Stages (OUT 6) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Ouroboros quadrature oscillator
patchIdeas.patch: Boot Stages with Mode 6 held for Ouroboros firmware · sliders set harmonics · OUT 1 and OUT 2 give 90°-shifted sines (base and quadrature) · mix pairs for multi-harmonic additive drones and stereo imaging
patchIdeas.diagram:
Stages:
* Firmware: Ouroboros (boot hold)

- Stages (OUT 1) -> Mixer (L)
- Stages (OUT 2) -> Mixer (R) // 90° quadrature
- Stages (OUT 3) -> Mixer (In 3) // higher harmonic
- Stages (OUT 6) -> Mixer (In 4)
